title: nepeta sorgerae
intro: Nepeta sorgerae, also known as Sorger's catmint, is a species of flowering plant in the mint family (Lamiaceae) that is native to Afghanistan. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, respiratory problems, and inflammation. The health benefits of Nepeta sorgerae include its antimicrobial, antispasmodic, and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues, ease respiratory congestion, and reduce inflammation. Nepeta sorgerae can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is often used in traditional medicine to promote relaxation, reduce stress, and improve overall well-being.
